Here's the content of /mnt/skills/public/pdf/SKILL.md with line numbers:
     1	---
     2	name: pdf
     3	description: Comprehensive PDF manipulation toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating new PDFs, merging/splitting documents, and handling forms. When Claude needs to fill in a PDF form or programmatically process, generate, or analyze PDF documents at scale.
     4	license: Proprietary. LICENSE.txt has complete terms
     5	---
     6	
     7	# PDF Processing Guide
     8	
     9	## Overview
    10	
    11	This guide covers essential PDF processing operations using Python libraries and command-line tools. For advanced features, JavaScript libraries, and detailed examples, see REFERENCE.md. If you need to fill out a PDF form, read FORMS.md and follow its instructions.
    12	
    13	## Quick Start
    14	
    15	```python
    16	from pypdf import PdfReader, PdfWriter
    17	
    18	# Read a PDF
    19	reader = PdfReader("document.pdf")
    20	print(f"Pages: {len(reader.pages)}")
    21	
    22	# Extract text
    23	text = ""
    24	for page in reader.pages:
    25	    text += page.extract_text()
    26	```
    27	
    28	## Python Libraries
    29	
    30	### pypdf - Basic Operations
    31	
    32	#### Merge PDFs
    33	```python
    34	from pypdf import PdfWriter, PdfReader
    35	
    36	writer = PdfWriter()
    37	for pdf_file in ["doc1.pdf", "doc2.pdf", "doc3.pdf"]:
    38	    reader = PdfReader(pdf_file)
    39	    for page in reader.pages:
    40	        writer.add_page(page)
    41	
    42	with open("merged.pdf", "wb") as output:
    43	    writer.write(output)
    44	```
    45	
    46	#### Split PDF
    47	```python
    48	reader = PdfReader("input.pdf")
    49	for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages):
    50	    writer = PdfWriter()
    51	    writer.add_page(page)
    52	    with open(f"page_{i+1}.pdf", "wb") as output:
    53	        writer.write(output)
    54	```
    55	
    56	#### Extract Metadata
    57	```python
    58	reader = PdfReader("document.pdf")
    59	meta = reader.metadata
    60	print(f"Title: {meta.title}")
    61	print(f"Author: {meta.author}")
    62	print(f"Subject: {meta.subject}")
    63	print(f"Creator: {meta.creator}")
    64	```
    65	
    66	#### Rotate Pages
    67	```python
    68	reader = PdfReader("input.pdf")
    69	writer = PdfWriter()
    70	
    71	page = reader.pages[0]
    72	page.rotate(90)  # Rotate 90 degrees clockwise
    73	writer.add_page(page)
    74	
    75	with open("rotated.pdf", "wb") as output:
    76	    writer.write(output)
    77	```
    78	
    79	### pdfplumber - Text and Table Extraction
    80	
    81	#### Extract Text with Layout
    82	```python
    83	import pdfplumber
    84	
    85	with pdfplumber.open("document.pdf") as pdf:
    86	    for page in pdf.pages:
    87	        text = page.extract_text()
    88	        print(text)
    89	```
    90	
    91	#### Extract Tables
    92	```python
    93	with pdfplumber.open("document.pdf") as pdf:
    94	    for i, page in enumerate(pdf.pages):
    95	        tables = page.extract_tables()
    96	        for j, table in enumerate(tables):
    97	            print(f"Table {j+1} on page {i+1}:")
    98	            for row in table:
    99	                print(row)
   100	```
   101	
   102	#### Advanced Table Extraction
   103	```python
   104	import pandas as pd
   105	
   106	with pdfplumber.open("document.pdf") as pdf:
   107	    all_tables = []
   108	    for page in pdf.pages:
   109	        tables = page.extract_tables()
   110	        for table in tables:
   111	            if table:  # Check if table is not empty
   112	                df = pd.DataFrame(table[1:], columns=table[0])
   113	                all_tables.append(df)
   114	
   115	# Combine all tables
   116	if all_tables:
   117	    combined_df = pd.concat(all_tables, ignore_index=True)
   118	    combined_df.to_excel("extracted_tables.xlsx", index=False)
   119	```
   120	
   121	### reportlab - Create PDFs
   122	
   123	#### Basic PDF Creation
   124	```python
   125	from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
   126	from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas
   127	
   128	c = canvas.Canvas("hello.pdf", pagesize=letter)
   129	width, height = letter
   130	
   131	# Add text
   132	c.drawString(100, height - 100, "Hello World!")
   133	c.drawString(100, height - 120, "This is a PDF created with reportlab")
   134	
   135	# Add a line
   136	c.line(100, height - 140, 400, height - 140)
   137	
   138	# Save
   139	c.save()
   140	```
   141	
   142	#### Create PDF with Multiple Pages
   143	```python
   144	from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
   145	from reportlab.platypus import SimpleDocTemplate, Paragraph, Spacer, PageBreak
   146	from reportlab.lib.styles import getSampleStyleSheet
   147	
   148	doc = SimpleDocTemplate("report.pdf", pagesize=letter)
   149	styles = getSampleStyleSheet()
   150	story = []
   151	
   152	# Add content
   153	title = Paragraph("Report Title", styles['Title'])
   154	story.append(title)
   155	story.append(Spacer(1, 12))
   156	
   157	body = Paragraph("This is the body of the report. " * 20, styles['Normal'])
   158	story.append(body)
   159	story.append(PageBreak())
   160	
   161	# Page 2
   162	story.append(Paragraph("Page 2", styles['Heading1']))
   163	story.append(Paragraph("Content for page 2", styles['Normal']))
   164	
   165	# Build PDF
   166	doc.build(story)
   167	```
   168	
   169	## Command-Line Tools
   170	
   171	### pdftotext (poppler-utils)
   172	```bash
   173	# Extract text
   174	pdftotext input.pdf output.txt
   175	
   176	# Extract text preserving layout
   177	pdftotext -layout input.pdf output.txt
   178	
   179	# Extract specific pages
   180	pdftotext -f 1 -l 5 input.pdf output.txt  # Pages 1-5
   181	```
   182	
   183	### qpdf
   184	```bash
   185	# Merge PDFs
   186	qpdf --empty --pages file1.pdf file2.pdf -- merged.pdf
   187	
   188	# Split pages
   189	qpdf input.pdf --pages . 1-5 -- pages1-5.pdf
   190	qpdf input.pdf --pages . 6-10 -- pages6-10.pdf
   191	
   192	# Rotate pages
   193	qpdf input.pdf output.pdf --rotate=+90:1  # Rotate page 1 by 90 degrees
   194	
   195	# Remove password
   196	qpdf --password=mypassword --decrypt encrypted.pdf decrypted.pdf
   197	```
   198	
   199	### pdftk (if available)
   200	```bash
   201	# Merge
   202	pdftk file1.pdf file2.pdf cat output merged.pdf
   203	
   204	# Split
   205	pdftk input.pdf burst
   206	
   207	# Rotate
   208	pdftk input.pdf rotate 1east output rotated.pdf
   209	```
   210	
   211	## Common Tasks
   212	
   213	### Extract Text from Scanned PDFs
   214	```python
   215	# Requires: pip install pytesseract pdf2image
   216	import pytesseract
   217	from pdf2image import convert_from_path
   218	
   219	# Convert PDF to images
   220	images = convert_from_path('scanned.pdf')
   221	
   222	# OCR each page
   223	text = ""
   224	for i, image in enumerate(images):
   225	    text += f"Page {i+1}:\n"
   226	    text += pytesseract.image_to_string(image)
   227	    text += "\n\n"
   228	
   229	print(text)
   230	```
   231	
   232	### Add Watermark
   233	```python
   234	from pypdf import PdfReader, PdfWriter
   235	
   236	# Create watermark (or load existing)
   237	watermark = PdfReader("watermark.pdf").pages[0]
   238	
   239	# Apply to all pages
   240	reader = PdfReader("document.pdf")
   241	writer = PdfWriter()
   242	
   243	for page in reader.pages:
   244	    page.merge_page(watermark)
   245	    writer.add_page(page)
   246	
   247	with open("watermarked.pdf", "wb") as output:
   248	    writer.write(output)
   249	```
   250	
   251	### Extract Images
   252	```bash
   253	# Using pdfimages (poppler-utils)
   254	pdfimages -j input.pdf output_prefix
   255	
   256	# This extracts all images as output_prefix-000.jpg, output_prefix-001.jpg, etc.
   257	```
   258	
   259	### Password Protection
   260	```python
   261	from pypdf import PdfReader, PdfWriter
   262	
   263	reader = PdfReader("input.pdf")
   264	writer = PdfWriter()
   265	
   266	for page in reader.pages:
   267	    writer.add_page(page)
   268	
   269	# Add password
   270	writer.encrypt("userpassword", "ownerpassword")
   271	
   272	with open("encrypted.pdf", "wb") as output:
   273	    writer.write(output)
   274	```
   275	
   276	## Quick Reference
   277	
   278	| Task | Best Tool | Command/Code |
   279	|------|-----------|--------------|
   280	| Merge PDFs | pypdf | `writer.add_page(page)` |
   281	| Split PDFs | pypdf | One page per file |
   282	| Extract text | pdfplumber | `page.extract_text()` |
   283	| Extract tables | pdfplumber | `page.extract_tables()` |
   284	| Create PDFs | reportlab | Canvas or Platypus |
   285	| Command line merge | qpdf | `qpdf --empty --pages ...` |
   286	| OCR scanned PDFs | pytesseract | Convert to image first |
   287	| Fill PDF forms | pdf-lib or pypdf (see FORMS.md) | See FORMS.md |

from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import landscape
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas
from reportlab.lib.colors import HexColor
import math

# Page setup - larger canvas for better spacing
PAGE_WIDTH = 1800
PAGE_HEIGHT = 1300

# Colors
DARK_BG = HexColor('#1a1a2e')
CORAL = HexColor('#ff6b6b')
CYAN = HexColor('#4ecdc4')
YELLOW = HexColor('#ffe66d')
PINK = HexColor('#ff8fab')
WHITE = HexColor('#ffffff')
LIGHT_GRAY = HexColor('#cccccc')

def draw_rounded_rect(c, x, y, width, height, radius, fill_color, stroke=False):
    """Draw a rounded rectangle"""
    c.setFillColor(fill_color)
    if stroke:
        c.setStrokeColor(WHITE)
        c.setLineWidth(1)
    else:
        c.setStrokeColor(fill_color)
    
    path = c.beginPath()
    path.moveTo(x + radius, y)
    path.lineTo(x + width - radius, y)
    path.arcTo(x + width - radius, y, x + width, y + radius, 270, 90)
    path.lineTo(x + width, y + height - radius)
    path.arcTo(x + width - radius, y + height - radius, x + width, y + height, 0, 90)
    path.lineTo(x + radius, y + height)
    path.arcTo(x, y + height - radius, x + radius, y + height, 90, 90)
    path.lineTo(x, y + radius)
    path.arcTo(x, y, x + radius, y + radius, 180, 90)
    path.close()
    c.drawPath(path, fill=1, stroke=stroke)

def draw_text_box(c, x, y, width, height, text, font_size, fill_color, text_color=None, bold=False):
    """Draw a text box with centered text"""
    if text_color is None:
        text_color = WHITE if fill_color in [CORAL, CYAN, PINK] else HexColor('#1a1a2e')
    
    draw_rounded_rect(c, x, y, width, height, 8, fill_color)
    
    font = "Helvetica-Bold" if bold else "Helvetica"
    c.setFont(font, font_size)
    c.setFillColor(text_color)
    
    lines = text.split('\n')
    line_height = font_size + 2
    total_height = len(lines) * line_height
    start_y = y + height/2 + total_height/2 - line_height/2
    
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        text_width = c.stringWidth(line, font, font_size)
        c.drawString(x + (width - text_width)/2, start_y - i * line_height, line)

def draw_small_box(c, x, y, width, height, text, fill_color, font_size=8):
    """Draw a small label box"""
    text_color = WHITE if fill_color in [CORAL, PINK] else HexColor('#1a1a2e')
    draw_rounded_rect(c, x, y, width, height, 5, fill_color)
    c.setFont("Helvetica", font_size)
    c.setFillColor(text_color)
    
    lines = text.split('\n')
    line_height = font_size + 1
    total_height = len(lines) * line_height
    start_y = y + height/2 + total_height/2 - line_height/2
    
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        text_width = c.stringWidth(line, "Helvetica", font_size)
        c.drawString(x + (width - text_width)/2, start_y - i * line_height, line)

def draw_connection(c, x1, y1, x2, y2, color=None):
    """Draw a curved connection line"""
    if color is None:
        color = HexColor('#4a4a6a')
    c.setStrokeColor(color)
    c.setLineWidth(2)
    
    # Draw a bezier curve
    mid_x = (x1 + x2) / 2
    c.bezier(x1, y1, mid_x, y1, mid_x, y2, x2, y2)

def create_mindmap():
    c = canvas.Canvas("/home/claude/jacksonville-mindmap-fixed.pdf", pagesize=(PAGE_WIDTH, PAGE_HEIGHT))
    
    # Background
    c.setFillColor(DARK_BG)
    c.rect(0, 0, PAGE_WIDTH, PAGE_HEIGHT, fill=1, stroke=0)
    
    # Title
    c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 32)
    c.setFillColor(WHITE)
    c.drawCentredString(PAGE_WIDTH/2, PAGE_HEIGHT - 50, "Jacksonville Design Community Strategy")
    
    c.setFont("Helvetica", 16)
    c.setFillColor(LIGHT_GRAY)
    c.drawCentredString(PAGE_WIDTH/2, PAGE_HEIGHT - 80, "Transforming into a Design Knowledge Powerhouse")
    
    # Center node
    center_x, center_y = PAGE_WIDTH/2, PAGE_HEIGHT/2
    draw_text_box(c, center_x - 80, center_y - 40, 160, 80, "Jacksonville\nDesign\nCommunity", 14, CORAL, bold=True)
    
    # Categories legend (top left)
    legend_x, legend_y = 50, PAGE_HEIGHT - 180
    c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 12)
    c.setFillColor(WHITE)
    c.drawString(legend_x, legend_y + 80, "CATEGORIES")
    
    categories = [
        (CORAL, "Strategy & Vision"),
        (CYAN, "Tools & Knowledge"),
        (YELLOW, "Community & Events"),
        (PINK, "Leadership & Growth")
    ]
    for i, (color, name) in enumerate(categories):
        draw_rounded_rect(c, legend_x, legend_y - i*25, 15, 15, 3, color)
        c.setFont("Helvetica", 10)
        c.setFillColor(WHITE)
        c.drawString(legend_x + 22, legend_y - i*25 + 3, name)
    
    # ============ THE PARADOX (right side) ============
    paradox_x, paradox_y = center_x + 280, center_y
    draw_text_box(c, paradox_x - 70, paradox_y - 30, 140, 60, "THE PARADOX", 12, CORAL, bold=True)
    draw_connection(c, center_x + 80, center_y, paradox_x - 70, paradox_y)
    
    # Paradox sub-items
    paradox_items = ["Learning from Atlanta", "Missing Connective Tissue", "Raw Ingredients Exist", "Infrastructure Focus", "Not About Friendship"]
    for i, item in enumerate(paradox_items):
        box_x = paradox_x + 100
        box_y = paradox_y + 80 - i * 38
        draw_small_box(c, box_x, box_y, 140, 28, item, CORAL, 9)
        draw_connection(c, paradox_x + 70, paradox_y, box_x, box_y + 14, CORAL)
    
    # ============ KNOWLEDGE CAPTURE (upper right) ============
    kc_x, kc_y = center_x + 200, center_y + 200
    draw_text_box(c, kc_x - 70, kc_y - 30, 140, 60, "KNOWLEDGE\nCAPTURE", 12, CYAN, bold=True)
    draw_connection(c, center_x + 60, center_y + 40, kc_x - 70, kc_y)
    
    # KC sub-items
    kc_items = [
        ("Explicit Knowledge:\nDocumentation, Case Studies,\nTemplates, Guidelines", 130, 50),
        ("Tacit Knowledge:\nIntuition, Relationships,\nPolitical Navigation", 130, 50),
        ("8-Stage Lifecycle:\nGenerate → Articulate → Capture\n→ Organize → Distribute\n→ Apply → Validate → Evolve", 160, 60)
    ]
    for i, (text, w, h) in enumerate(kc_items):
        box_x = kc_x + 100
        box_y = kc_y + 100 - i * 65
        draw_small_box(c, box_x, box_y, w, h, text, CYAN, 8)
        draw_connection(c, kc_x + 70, kc_y, box_x, box_y + h/2, CYAN)
    
    # ============ TOOLS & PLATFORMS (upper center) ============
    tp_x, tp_y = center_x - 50, center_y + 280
    draw_text_box(c, tp_x - 60, tp_y - 25, 120, 50, "TOOLS &\nPLATFORMS", 12, CYAN, bold=True)
    draw_connection(c, center_x, center_y + 40, tp_x, tp_y - 25)
    
    # TP sub-items
    tp_items = [
        ("Slack:\nCommunity Nervous System\n25+ Channels", -180, 50),
        ("Notion:\nMember Directory\nCase Studies\nEvent Management", -180, -20),
        ("Content Platforms:\nNewsletter, Social Media,\nPublic Presence", 80, 50)
    ]
    for text, dx, dy in tp_items:
        box_x = tp_x + dx
        box_y = tp_y + dy
        draw_small_box(c, box_x, box_y, 130, 55, text, CYAN, 8)
        draw_connection(c, tp_x, tp_y, box_x + 65, box_y + 27, CYAN)
    
    # ============ EVENT FORMATS (upper left) ============
    ef_x, ef_y = center_x - 250, center_y + 220
    draw_text_box(c, ef_x - 60, ef_y - 25, 120, 50, "EVENT\nFORMATS", 12, YELLOW, bold=True)
    draw_connection(c, center_x - 60, center_y + 30, ef_x + 60, ef_y)
    
    # EF sub-items
    ef_items = [
        "Unconferences:\nParticipant-Driven Agendas",
        "Design Critiques:\nPeer Feedback Sessions",
        "Workshops:\nHands-On Skill Building",
        "Lightning Talks:\n5-Minute Presentations",
        "Portfolio Reviews &\nCoffee Chats"
    ]
    for i, text in enumerate(ef_items):
        box_x = ef_x - 180
        box_y = ef_y + 100 - i * 50
        draw_small_box(c, box_x, box_y, 140, 40, text, YELLOW, 8)
        draw_connection(c, ef_x - 60, ef_y, box_x + 140, box_y + 20, YELLOW)
    
    # ============ COMMUNITY BUILDING (left side) ============
    cb_x, cb_y = center_x - 320, center_y
    draw_text_box(c, cb_x - 70, cb_y - 30, 140, 60, "COMMUNITY\nBUILDING", 12, YELLOW, bold=True)
    draw_connection(c, center_x - 80, center_y, cb_x + 70, cb_y)
    
    # CB sub-items
    cb_items = ["Low-Barrier Entry", "Public Recognition", "Feedback Loops", "Peer Mentorship", "Celebrate Wins", "Exceptional Onboarding"]
    for i, item in enumerate(cb_items):
        box_x = cb_x - 170
        box_y = cb_y + 80 - i * 32
        draw_small_box(c, box_x, box_y, 130, 24, item, YELLOW, 9)
        draw_connection(c, cb_x - 70, cb_y, box_x + 130, box_y + 12, YELLOW)
    
    # ============ PARTNERSHIPS (lower left) ============
    ps_x, ps_y = center_x - 280, center_y - 200
    draw_text_box(c, ps_x - 70, ps_y - 25, 140, 50, "PARTNERSHIPS", 12, YELLOW, bold=True)
    draw_connection(c, center_x - 60, center_y - 30, ps_x + 70, ps_y)
    
    # PS sub-items
    ps_items = [
        ("Corporate Partners:\nTalent Pipeline\nEvent Sponsorship", -150, 30),
        ("Universities:\nStudent Connections\nGuest Lectures\nInternship Programs", -150, -50),
        ("Mutual Value:\nDesign Maturity Assessment\nRecruitment Access", 50, -60)
    ]
    for text, dx, dy in ps_items:
        box_x = ps_x + dx
        box_y = ps_y + dy
        draw_small_box(c, box_x, box_y, 130, 55, text, YELLOW, 8)
        draw_connection(c, ps_x, ps_y, box_x + 65, box_y + 27, YELLOW)
    
    # ============ LEADERSHIP STRUCTURE (lower center) ============
    ls_x, ls_y = center_x, center_y - 250
    draw_text_box(c, ls_x - 70, ls_y - 25, 140, 50, "LEADERSHIP\nSTRUCTURE", 12, PINK, bold=True)
    draw_connection(c, center_x, center_y - 40, ls_x, ls_y + 25)
    
    # LS sub-items
    ls_items = [
        ("Distributed Leadership", -180, 10),
        ("Clear Roles:\nArchitect • Operations", -180, -40),
        ("Succession Planning", 90, 10),
        ("Leadership Development", 90, -40)
    ]
    for text, dx, dy in ls_items:
        box_x = ls_x + dx
        box_y = ls_y + dy
        h = 35 if '\n' in text else 24
        draw_small_box(c, box_x, box_y, 130, h, text, PINK, 8)
        draw_connection(c, ls_x, ls_y, box_x + 65, box_y + h/2, PINK)
    
    # ============ METRICS & GROWTH (lower right) ============
    mg_x, mg_y = center_x + 220, center_y - 180
    draw_text_box(c, mg_x - 60, mg_y - 25, 120, 50, "METRICS &\nGROWTH", 12, PINK, bold=True)
    draw_connection(c, center_x + 60, center_y - 30, mg_x - 60, mg_y)
    
    # MG sub-items
    mg_items = ["Active Members", "Event Attendance", "Knowledge Contributions", "Career Advancements", "Impact Stories", "Community Satisfaction"]
    for i, item in enumerate(mg_items):
        box_x = mg_x + 80
        box_y = mg_y + 70 - i * 28
        draw_small_box(c, box_x, box_y, 135, 22, item, PINK, 8)
        draw_connection(c, mg_x + 60, mg_y, box_x, box_y + 11, PINK)
    
    # ============ IMPLEMENTATION TIMELINE (right bottom) ============
    it_x, it_y = PAGE_WIDTH - 220, 280
    draw_text_box(c, it_x - 90, it_y - 20, 180, 40, "IMPLEMENTATION TIMELINE", 11, PINK, bold=True)
    
    timeline_items = [
        "IMMEDIATE (2 weeks):\n• Convene leadership team\n• Set up Slack & Notion",
        "SHORT-TERM (3 months):\n• Launch knowledge repository\n• Host 3-4 events",
        "MEDIUM-TERM (6-12 months):\n• Secure 3+ partnerships\n• Host major unconference",
        "LONG-TERM (12+ months):\n• Establish regional brand\n• Sustainable funding model"
    ]
    for i, text in enumerate(timeline_items):
        box_y = it_y - 55 - i * 55
        draw_small_box(c, it_x - 90, box_y, 180, 50, text, PINK, 7)
    
    # ============ GLOBAL INSIGHTS (left bottom) ============
    gi_x, gi_y = 150, 200
    draw_text_box(c, gi_x - 100, gi_y + 60, 200, 35, "GLOBAL INSIGHTS", 12, CORAL, bold=True)
    
    c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 9)
    c.setFillColor(CORAL)
    c.drawString(gi_x - 95, gi_y + 45, "WORLD-CLASS COMMUNITIES:")
    
    c.setFont("Helvetica", 8)
    c.setFillColor(WHITE)
    communities = [
        "• SF Bay Area: Tech design hub",
        "• London: Design heritage",
        "• Tokyo: Craft & detail",
        "• Stockholm: Human-centered"
    ]
    for i, text in enumerate(communities):
        c.drawString(gi_x - 95, gi_y + 30 - i * 12, text)
    
    c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 9)
    c.setFillColor(CORAL)
    c.drawString(gi_x - 95, gi_y - 25, "KEY SUCCESS FACTORS:")
    
    c.setFont("Helvetica", 8)
    c.setFillColor(WHITE)
    factors = [
        "1. Education institutions",
        "2. Design-investing companies",
        "3. Knowledge connectors",
        "4. Visibility platforms",
        "5. Design advocates"
    ]
    for i, text in enumerate(factors):
        c.drawString(gi_x - 95, gi_y - 40 - i * 12, text)
    
    c.setFont("Helvetica-Bold", 9)
    c.setFillColor(YELLOW)
    c.drawString(gi_x - 95, gi_y - 110, "Jacksonville can build all 5")
    
    # Footer
    c.setFont("Helvetica", 10)
    c.setFillColor(LIGHT_GRAY)
    c.drawCentredString(PAGE_WIDTH/2, 30, "Jacksonville UI/UX Design Leadership Group • December 2024 • Comprehensive Strategy Guide")
    
    c.save()
    print("Mind map created successfully!")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    create_mindmap()

CYAN = HexColor('#4ecdc4')
YELLOW = HexColor('#ffe66d')
PINK = HexColor('#ff8fab')
WHITE = HexColor('#ffffff')
LIGHT_GRAY = HexColor('#cccccc')

def draw_rounded_rect(c, x, y, width, height, radius, fill_color, stroke=False):
    """Draw a rounded rectangle"""
    c.setFillColor(fill_color)
    if stroke:
        c.setStrokeColor(WHITE)
        c.setLineWidth(1)
    else:
        c.setStrokeColor(fill_color)
    
    path = c.beginPath()
    path.moveTo(x + radius, y)
    path.lineTo(x + width - radius, y)
    path.arcTo(x + width - radius, y, x + width, y + radius, 270, 90)
    path.lineTo(x + width, y + height - radius)
    path.arcTo(x + width - radius, y + height - radius, x + width, y + height, 0, 90)
    path.lineTo(x + radius, y + height)
    path.arcTo(x, y + height - radius, x + radius, y + height, 90, 90)
    path.lineTo(x, y + radius)
    path.arcTo(x, y, x + radius, y + radius, 180, 90)
    path.close()
    c.drawPath(path, fill=1, stroke=stroke)

def draw_text_box(c, x, y, width, height, text, font_size, fill_color, text_color=None, bold=False):
    """Draw a text box with centered text"""
    if text_color is None:
        text_color = WHITE if fill_color in [CORAL, CYAN, PINK] else HexColor('#1a1a2e')
    
    draw_rounded_rect(c, x, y, width, height, 8, fill_color)
    
    font = "Helvetica-Bold" if bold else "Helvetica"
    c.setFont(font, font_size)
    c.setFillColor(text_color)
    
    lines = text.split('\n')
    line_height = font_size + 2
    total_height = len(lines) * line_height
    start_y = y + height/2 + total_height/2 - line_height/2
    
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        text_width = c.stringWidth(line, font, font_size)
        c.drawString(x + (width - text_width)/2, start_y - i * line_height, line)

def draw_small_box(c, x, y, width, height, text, fill_color, font_size=8):
    """Draw a small label box"""
    text_color = WHITE if fill_color in [CORAL, PINK] else HexColor('#1a1a2e')
    draw_rounded_rect(c, x, y, width, height, 5, fill_color)
    c.setFont("Helvetica", font_size)
    c.setFillColor(text_color)
    
    lines = text.split('\n')
    line_height = font_size + 1
    total_height = len(lines) * line_height
    start_y = y + height/2 + total_height/2 - line_height/2
    
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        text_width = c.stringWidth(line, "Helvetica", font_size)
        c.drawString(x + (width - text_width)/2, start_y - i * line_height, line)

def draw_connection(c, x1, y1, x2, y2, color=None):
    """Draw a curved connection line"""
    if color is None:
        color = HexColor('#4a4a6a')
    c.setStrokeColor(color)
    c.setLineWidth(2)
    
    # Draw a bezier curve
    mid_x = (x1 + x2) / 2
    c.bezier(x1, y1, mid_x, y1, mid_x, y2, x2, y2)
